title: What language is this in
checkLabels.t3: The encoding must still be there
checkLabels.t4: The page name is still there
why.lost-content: Content vanished from the page. The task is to add the language, not to delete things.
why.empty: The editor is empty. You are adding one attribute, and you deleted the whole page. Bring the code back.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
why.no-lang: There is no lang. A screen reader does not know what language it is reading and will pronounce your text in the wrong voice.
why.empty-lang: lang is there, but it is empty. An empty attribute is the same as a missing one — nobody knows what the language is.
why.lost-meta: The encoding is gone. You are adding an attribute, not deleting lines.
hint2.no-lang: The attribute goes INSIDE the opening <html> tag, before the closing bracket.
hint3.no-lang: Look at the example. The tag is already there — you are only adding something to it.
hint2.empty-lang: The value has to be the language code. bg for Bulgarian, en for English.
hint3.empty-lang: If your page is in English — <html lang="en">. If it is in Bulgarian — <html lang="bg">.
blocks.0.text: Your page is in some language. You know that. The browser does not.
blocks.1.text: It seems pointless. Who cares what language it is in? Turns out — quite a few people.
blocks.3.text: It is not a new tag. It is something you write INSIDE a tag you already have.
blocks.5.text: Add it to your <html>. Run. Nothing changes on screen.
blocks.7.text: A screen reader. It needs to know how to pronounce the words. Without lang it will read your text in the wrong voice — and out comes a mess nobody understands.
blocks.8.text: Google too. It decides who to show your page to. If it does not know the language, it will show it to the wrong people, or to nobody.
blocks.9.text: "Think of the last time you opened a site and something suddenly started talking. What did you do? Closed the tab. Now imagine that reader is the only way you can read."
